description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that caches the Gradle home directory at $HOME/.gradle and defines three stages: build, test, and release. In the build stage add a jdk11 job using the openjdk:11 image that runs `./gradlew -i -s assemble --scan`, and a jdk8 job using openjdk:8 that runs `./gradlew build -i -s -x gradleTest --scan`; both jobs store the `.gradle/` and `build/` directories as artifacts for 60 minutes and are excluded from the pages pipeline. In the test stage add three jobs named test:gradle3, test:gradle4 and test:gradle5, each using openjdk:8, depending on jdk8, and executing `./gradlew -i -s gradleTest -DgradleTest.versions=... --scan` with the respective Gradle version sets (3.0,3.3,3.5; 4.0.2,4.2.1,4.9; 5.0-rc-4), also excluded from pages. In the release stage add a pages job that runs `ls -la`, publishes the `public` directory as an artifact and runs only on the pages pipeline, and a publish job that depends on jdk8, uses openjdk:8, runs `./gradlew installDocs publishPlugins gitPublishPush -i` with the Gradle publishing and Git credentials supplied via `$GRADLE_PORTAL_KEY`, `$GRADLE_PORTAL_SECRET`, `$PAGES_PUBLISH_USER`, `$PAGES_PUBLISH_KEY`, and forces hardcoded auth, and runs only on release.
